architecture_fit.note: Single uniform attention readout (delta from base_model) where max emerges from an internal log-sum-exp soft-max; genuinely solves OR with no query branching.

baseline_comparison.note: Plain-linear superposition strawman fails at low cos (0.707) where the OR circuit holds ~0.98; gap is shown directly in Panel A.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic circuit (stated explicitly) with self-ablations (no-softmax->~0.5, no-gate->leakage up) and a proposed real-model patching check; appropriate causal evidence for a hand-built mechanism.

operating_range.note: Flat sharpness across full cos sweep (robustness ~1.0) plus a beta sweep over >2 orders of magnitude tracing average->hard-max.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set circuit, no training; the 0.9827 vs 1.0 offset is exactly log(0.5)/beta, showing precise understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Three focused panels, each isolating one claim (sharpness-vs-cos, ablation bars, beta knob); legible without the README.

visualisation_rationale.note: Why-section ties each panel to the goal, picks the right failing strawman, and corrects pass_3's misuse of the oracle baseline.

notes: A hand-built log-sum-exp soft-max attention circuit that produces OR/max-pooling from softmax's own internal operation, with a single uniform callable (no branching) reaching ~0.98 canonical sharpness flat across the cosine sweep. Strong strawman comparison, component ablations, a wide beta operating range, and a fully hardcoded mechanism whose 0.98 offset is explained exactly. Only minor weaknesses: noise leakage sits ~0.4 and faithfulness is necessarily self-referential since there is no trained model.
